task_name: Novel Posterior Cul-de-Sac Simulation Model - Educational Presentation
analysis_summary: This is an educational/instructional video presenting a novel surgical simulation model for laparoscopic posterior cul-de-sac dissection training. The video contains presentation slides, materials lists, step-by-step construction instructions, and demonstration footage of the model in use within a laparoscopic trainer box. It is NOT an FLS official task performance video and therefore does NOT receive an FLS score.
reason_not_scored: This video is instructional content documenting the design, materials, construction methodology, and validation of a custom simulation model. It is not a performance recording of an FLS-certified task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture). The frames show: (1) presentation slides with background and objectives, (2) materials table and surgical tools list, (3) step-by-step model construction (uterosacral ligaments, painting, suturing through fundus and rectum, adhesion creation), (4) assembly into a laparoscopic trainer, and (5) validation results from 17 residents and 9 experts. No trainee is performing a timed FLS task.

video_segments_identified.content: Novel Posterior Cul-de-Sac Simulation Model by Senapati, Alsaden, Schroer, Tu

Problem statement: obliteration of posterior cul-de-sac is unexpected surgical finding; lack of simulation models; objectives: create realistic, affordable, reproducible model for residency curriculum

Detailed cost breakdown of materials totaling ~$3-4 per model: chair pad (uterus), fiberfill (endometriosis), mylar blankets (rectal mucosa), quilt batting (rectal serosa), paint, dyes, adhesives, twine
Construction of parametrial ligaments using twine with specific 0.5" and 2.5" spacing dimensions
Painting uterus pink using Liquitex acrylic paint; marking dissection planes (Level 1, 2, 3) with 1" and 0.5" spacing
Placement of sutures through uterine fundus with coiled twine extending externally
Assembly of rectal component using Mylar blankets (mucosa), fiberfill (serosa), cotton quilt batting; dimensions 5"×11" with 6" sections; adhesive assembly with spray glue
Placement of sutures through rectal component with external leads; shows completed model with suture placement marks
Assembly into trainer box with camera, light source, laparoscopic tower, and monitor; instruction to allow 24-hour drying; application of additional adhesive if needed

17 residents (9 PGY 1/2, 8 PGY 3/4) and 9 experts (4 GYN surgery fellows, 5 oncology/MIS attendings); pre/post surveys evaluating comfort level; 2-minute instructional video; 30 minutes allotted for dissection; results show increased comfort after one dissection; high realism and utility ratings
Model can be incorporated into resident training as affordable and realistic simulation model

Video clips (5 frames) showing the completed model inside a laparoscopic trainer box viewed via camera; demonstrates anatomic accuracy with colored foam components (pink uterus, green adhesions, beige rectum); shows laparoscopic instrument interaction; includes warning text 'DO NOT CUT THE BOWEL'

demonstrated_techniques: Low-cost construction of anatomically realistic posterior cul-de-sac model
Use of household materials (chair padding, fiberfill, mylar, batting) to simulate pelvic anatomy
Color-coding and marking of dissection planes for educational reference
Integration of adhesion simulation using spray adhesive and fiberfill
Suture placement technique for defining anatomic landmarks
Assembly of modular components into integrated trainer box setup
Importance of allowing adequate drying time (24 hours) before use
verbal_cues: Portions of the following video clip... (narration overlay at start)
DO NOT CUT THE BOWEL (red warning text during laparoscopic demonstration)
common_errors_addressed: Model fragility: importance of 24-hour drying and proper adhesive application
Anatomic confusion: clear level marking and color differentiation to prevent disorientation during dissection
Bowel injury: explicit warning against cutting rectal component during laparoscopic practice
teaching_content.educational_value: High — comprehensive documentation of simulation model development process from conceptualization through validation, enabling reproducibility and adoption by other training programs

validation_summary.study_design: Pre/post survey with instructional video (2 minutes) and 30-minute dissection session
outcomes: Increased comfort level after single dissection
High realism ratings from both residents and experts
High utility ratings for training purposes

scoring_determination.reason: This is a research presentation and educational tutorial on simulation model construction and validation, not a performance-based assessment of an FLS task. No trainee is executing Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture within a timed, scored protocol.
